en: Please, Ross. It was so obvious.
ko: 더 이상 사무실에서 네 영역을 표시하듯이.

en: It was like you were marking your territory. I mean, you
might as well have just come in and peed all around my desk.
ko: 그냥 들어와서 내 책상 주위에 소변이나 쌌으면 좋았을 텐데.

en: I would never do that.
I know what's going on here, okay?
ko: 여기서 무슨 일이 일어나고 있는지 알아, 알겠지?

en: Mark explained it all to me.
He said, this is what you guys do.
Yeah, well, if Mark said that, then Mark's an idiot.
Mark's a genius.
Why? How? How is he a genius?
ko: 왜? 어떻게? 그가 천재인 이유가 뭐야?

en: Look, don't you see what's happening here? Instead of hitting on her right away, he's
ko: 여기서 무슨 일이 일어나고 있는지 모르겠어? 바로 작업을 거는 대신,

en: becoming her confidant.
Now he's going to be the guy that she goes to to complain about you.
ko: 이제 그녀는 너에 대해 불평할 사람으로 그를 찾을 거야.

en: What am I going to do?
Well, why don't you send her a musical bug?
ko: 그녀에게 뮤지컬 벌레를 보내는 건 어때?

en: Oh, no, you already did that.
All right, look, you're going to have to go there yourself now, okay?
ko: 자, 이제 너가 직접 가야 해

en: Make a few surprise
visits. I don't know you guys.
ko: 몇 번 놀라운 방문을 해봐, 난 너희가 어떻게 할지 모르겠어.

en: All right, fine. Don't do anything.
ko: 자, 좋아, 아무것도 하지 말고 여기 앉아서 이야기해.

en: Just sit here and talk to us. Meanwhile, she is talking
to him about you, and he's being Mr. Joe sensitive, and she's...
ko: 그동안 그녀는 너에 대해 그와 이야기하고 있고 그는 아주 감성적이야.
